title: Author PROP-071 — audit all 13 scheduled agent prompts for FUSE-relative reads of state files that should come from clone; eliminate the per-session FUSE staleness bug class (tinker FND-01 + baby tracker observation are two known instances)
background:
Per-session FUSE staleness is now a confirmed-recurring bug class. Two known instances surfaced today, both with the same shape:

INSTANCE 1 (tinker, fixed 2026-05-31T11:51Z commit 787ee3892f): tinker's pre-flight Operator-Directive Discovery scan read `monitor/tinker/operator-directives/` via relative path (FUSE), causing it to see status='pending' for directives that were already 'completed' in git from earlier runs in different sessions. PROP-066 Step 4c only syncs FUSE within the session it runs in; other agents' sessions have independently stale FUSEs. Operator patched tinker.md to read directives from a fresh clone or via the GitHub Contents API. Same lesson now needs to be propagated.

INSTANCE 2 (analyst-baby, observed 2026-05-31T~15:00Z): Baby's Mode 1 dispatcher reads `monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json` via a relative path at analyst-baby.md line 84. CWD at that point is the per-session FUSE workspace (/sessions/<baby-session-id>/mnt/dome-model-review). A baby run observed 10 pending tracker items while git showed 3 — pure FUSE lag, no re-work performed but signals the bug exists. Baby DOES maintain a CLEAN_CLONE (refreshed via Step 0a's git pull --rebase) but uses the FUSE-relative path for the tracker read instead of `${CLEAN_CLONE}/monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json`. The clone has fresh data; the read reaches into stale FUSE.

Operator's expectation when filing this: 'FUSE shouldn't have been stale. there were 2 workspace sync runs between those 2 baby runs.' This reflects an assumption that workspace-sync's git→FUSE sync propagates cross-session. It does NOT — workspace-sync's PROP-066 Step 4c invokes `node build.js sync-workspace` which writes to ${WORKSPACE} (workspace-sync's own session FUSE). Baby's session FUSE is a distinct mount; workspace-sync cannot reach it (per the same per-session UID isolation barrier that defeated PROP-067's cross-session sweep).

This is an architectural limitation, not a bug in PROP-066. The fix shape is per-agent: any agent that reads state files via a FUSE-relative path needs to instead read from its own fresh clone (or via GitHub API). Tinker has been fixed; baby and possibly others have not.
operator_question_to_resolve: Does cowork's per-session FUSE backend have any cross-session freshness mechanism we have not yet documented? The empirical evidence from today (tinker FND-01 + baby tracker observation) points to 'no — each session FUSE is independently initialized and only PROP-066 Step 4c keeps the running session's own FUSE current'. If tinker's investigation surfaces a different mechanism (e.g., session-init pulls origin/main HEAD, FUSE files auto-refresh after N minutes, etc.), PROP-071 should document it. If the empirical model holds, PROP-071's fix scope is per-agent prompt edits.
requested_work:
Step 1 — Read tinker.md, analyst.md, analyst-baby.md, decider.md, curmudgeon.md, curmudgeon-verify.md, integrity/structure-integrity.md, social.md, poller.md, sloppytoppy-score.md, sloppytoppy-rewrite.md, workspace-sync.md, and the prune-integrity SKILL.md (script-only). For each, identify ALL `fs.readFileSync(...)`, `JSON.parse(...)`, `git -C ...`, `cat ...`, or equivalent reads of state files. Categorize each read by:
  (a) source path: FUSE-relative (e.g., `'monitor/...'`), FUSE-absolute (`${WORKSPACE}/...`), CLEAN_CLONE-relative, CLONE-relative, or other clone path
  (b) file class: STATE_FILE (open-issues, expansion-tracker, priority-queue, attention-inbox, closed-issues, latest-*-summary.txt) — must be fresh; CONFIG (rubric-config, math-dense-surfaces) — slow-changing, FUSE-OK; REFERENCE (prompts, reference/, CLAUDE.md) — slow-changing, FUSE-OK; PER_SESSION (status.json, run-state.json) — session-local, no staleness possible.
  (c) intent: read-only inspection vs read-modify-write (mutates require fresh state)

Step 2 — Produce a per-agent audit table. For each agent: list every state-file read with its (path, class, intent, current source). Flag rows where source=FUSE-relative AND class=STATE_FILE AND intent=read-modify-write or important-read. These are the bugs. Flag rows where source=FUSE-relative AND class=STATE_FILE AND intent=read-only-inspection as MEDIUM (may cause stale-decision but not stale-write).

Step 3 — Confirm or refute the per-session-FUSE-independence hypothesis. Methodology: from a workspace-sync session A's bash, write a known marker file to ${WORKSPACE}/_TEST-PROP-071-marker. From a separate scheduled-agent invocation (e.g., the next tinker run), check if that marker file is visible in tinker's ${WORKSPACE}. If yes: cross-session FUSE has some shared mechanism. If no: per-session FUSE confirmed. Document the finding. (If you can't safely run this test cross-session, simulate it by checking whether any past inter-session FUSE writes have been visible to subsequent sessions — look for stale-detection events in tinker reports as natural experiments.)

Step 4 — For each flagged read from Step 2, recommend ONE of three fixes:
  (a) READ_FROM_CLONE: change the read path to `${CLEAN_CLONE}/...` or equivalent agent-specific clone path. Mirror the tinker.md FND-01 fix pattern (commit 787ee3892f).
  (b) READ_VIA_API: use GitHub Contents API to read the file from origin/main HEAD directly. More API calls but doesn't require local clone. Best for agents that don't otherwise need a clone.
  (c) NO_FIX_NEEDED: the read is FUSE-relative but the class is CONFIG/REFERENCE/PER_SESSION (not STATE_FILE), so staleness is acceptable. Document why.
For each, include the exact line number and a suggested replacement snippet.

Step 5 — Author PROP-071 with: (a) Step 3's finding on cross-session FUSE behavior, (b) the per-agent audit table from Step 2, (c) recommended fixes from Step 4, (d) operator-side observability: how do we detect future staleness regressions (e.g., add a stale-tracker check to integrity that compares FUSE tracker count vs origin/main tracker count and reports drift), (e) optional architectural follow-up question for the operator (e.g., 'should we explore whether cowork session-init can be configured to pull origin/main HEAD before agent startup, which would close the entire bug class architecturally').

Step 6 — Lifecycle: mark this directive completed with prop_id_authored='PROP-071'. Implementation is a follow-up directive after operator review of the per-agent audit (some recommended fixes may need operator design calls).
non_goals: Do NOT IMPLEMENT PROP-071 fixes in this run — author only. Per-agent prompt patches land in a separate implementation directive after operator reviews the audit table.
Do NOT re-fix tinker.md FND-01 — already done at commit 787ee3892f. Just confirm in the audit that tinker's directive discovery now reads from clone/API.
Do NOT touch workspace-sync's PROP-066 Step 4c logic — it correctly syncs the session it runs in. The bug is NOT in PROP-066; it's in OTHER agents reading from FUSE.
Do NOT add a system-level cross-session FUSE sync mechanism — out of scope for the dome codebase (would need cowork infrastructure changes).
Do NOT supersede or amend PROP-066. PROP-071 is complementary, addressing a different surface (consumer-side reads vs producer-side writes).

context_pointers.tinker_fnd_01_fix: monitor/prompts/tinker.md, commit 787ee3892f (2026-05-31T11:51Z) — added 'CRITICAL — read directives from the git clone, NOT FUSE' block at Pre-flight Operator Directive Discovery
context_pointers.baby_tracker_read_bug: monitor/prompts/analyst-baby.md line 84 — `fs.readFileSync('monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json','utf8')` with relative-path CWD = FUSE workspace
context_pointers.baby_clean_clone_setup: monitor/prompts/analyst-baby.md line 218 — CLEAN_CLONE is set up but tracker read at line 84 doesn't use it
context_pointers.prop_066_helper: monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js — syncs git→FUSE for workspace-sync's session only; this is correct behavior, not a bug to fix
context_pointers.prop_034_phase_1: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-034-analyst-baby.json — defines baby's read/write contract for expansion-tracker
context_pointers.claude_md_per_session_fuse: CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules — implicit per-session-FUSE model (each scheduled task has its own /sessions/<id>/mnt/dome-model-review FUSE mount)
context_pointers.operator_intent_verbatim: 'FUSE shouldn't have been stale. there were 2 workspace sync runs between those 2 baby runs.' — Operator confirmed in cowork session 2026-05-31T15:00Z, reflecting the architectural assumption that workspace-sync propagates cross-session. The empirical finding is that it does not.

expected_output: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-071-fuse-staleness-audit.json (status='design-pending-operator-review') containing: per-agent audit table (Step 2), Step 3 architectural finding on per-session FUSE behavior, fix recommendations per flagged read (Step 4), operator-side observability proposal (Step 5d), optional follow-up question on architectural cowork-level fix (Step 5e).
estimated_effort: ~45-60 min Opus. Step 1 (read 13 prompts + categorize reads) is ~20-25 min. Step 2-3 (table + cross-session test) is ~10-15 min. Step 4-5 (recommendations + author) is ~15 min.
priority_note: MEDIUM — bug class with observable instances (2 today, possibly more lurking). Operational impact varies: stale tracker reads cause baby to skip-eligible work or re-claim already-handled items (low-grade noise so far, no data loss). Stale state-decision reads (decider, curmudgeon) could cause more serious issues. The value of PROP-071: (a) fixes the visible bugs, (b) eliminates the bug class via systematic audit, (c) potentially surfaces an architectural follow-up if Step 5e finds a cowork-level fix path.
